entry.title: Q1 2026 Quarterly Release Highlights: SAP BTP Security and Identity & Access Management
entry.content:
Another quarter done and dusted! We kicked off the new year with several new features and capabilities.

For a complete overview of feature deliveries for SAP Cloud Identity Services, check out our list of all new feature announcements in the SAP Cloud Identity Services Release Notes on the SAP Help Portal.

SAP Cloud Identity Services: Multiple corporate identity providers with the same issuer

In large enterprise environments, a single corporate identity provider often can't satisfy every trust requirement — especially when different business units have conflicting needs. Based on a customer improvement request, SAP Cloud Identity Services now supports multiple OpenID Connect corporate IdP configurations sharing the same issuer URL, giving teams the flexibility they need. For more information, see SAP Note 3718653 

SAP Cloud Identity Services: Authorization Management Service API for customer-specific policies

The Authorization Management (AMS) component of SAP Cloud Identity Services now offers an API that allows you to maintain customer-specific policies. This is a meaningful step toward automating governance and reducing manual overhead for security teams. For general information on AMS, please check the SAP Cloud Identity Services Developer Guide.  

SAP Custom Domain Service: New REST API for automatic certificate renewal

Managing domain certificates manually is error-prone — and with certificate lifespans soon set to drop to 90 days or less, automation becomes essential. The SAP Custom Domain Service now includes a REST API that handles certificate renewal automatically. It allows customers to build an integration with both on-premise and cloud-based Certificate Lifecycle Management processes. For details, please check the SAP Help Portal.

Stay in the loop

Join the SAP BTP Security and SAP Cloud Identity Services communities to get updates, share feedback, and connect with others!

entry.title: Re: A method for deploying made-to-order (MTO) items with...
entry.content:
Dear @shiwa,

For Make-to-Order / strategy 20, it is not possible to generate the finished-product individual customer requirement in a storage-location MRP area. SAP explicitly states that for MTO and ETO, planning can only be performed in the plant MRP area and that individual customer segments are always in the plant MRP area, even if a storage location assigned in the sales order refers to a storage-location MRP area

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3485915

For your second question the supported workaround is on the component side, not on the finished-product customer segment side. SAP documents that procurement elements at storage locations covered by MRP lead to dependent requirements for components and SAP also confirms that in manufacturing the material components required for production are contained in the planned order and when the planned order is converted the dependent requirements for the components are converted into reservations. This means you can still use the MRP-area / storage-location setup to support component planning and staging, even though the strategy-20 finished-product demand remains in the plant MRP area

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/a05fbd534f22b44ce10000000a174cb4.html

https://help.sap.com/doc/7c9e0bbbd1664c2581b2038a1c7ae4b3/2602.500/en-US/FSD_CE2602.pdf

So the practical Public Cloud design is:

  • keep the FG with strategy 20 planned in the plant MRP area
  • use the relevant MRP area / storage-location setup for the components
  • let the BOM explosion and subsequent planned order / order conversion drive the component dependent requirements and reservations
  • use the assigned manufacturing storage location for component issue/staging execution, not to relocate the MTO customer segment itself

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/d2abce5314894208e10000000a174cb4.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/6b10b8535c39b44ce10000000a174cb4.html

Summary:

For strategy 20 the finished-product MTO requirement cannot be generated in a storage-location MRP area in standard Public Cloud. The SAP-supported approach is to keep the FG demand in the plant MRP area and use the MRP area for component-level planning / dependent requirements / staging

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: 1H 2026 Release: Streamlining Data after Employment Terminations in SuccessFactors Employee Central
entry.content:
Streamlining Data after Employment Terminations in SuccessFactors Employee Central 

Managing data after an employment termination just became easier.

SAP SuccessFactors now automatically deletes future‑dated Job Information and Compensation Information records (including recurring pay components) after an employment is terminated. Once configured, this enhancement reduces manual cleanup, improves data consistency, and works seamlessly across UI, APIs, and imports.

Designed for HR admins, this feature helps keep Employee Central data clean and reliable, by default, with full administrative control.

This new capability is designed with real‑world HR operations in mind, especially in environments where future changes are planned in advance. The feature is inspired by the Customer Influence idea Customer Influence Idea 249640.

The Challenge

In many organizations, employments have future‑dated Job Information and Compensation Information records, including recurring pay components, already scheduled.

When an employment is terminated, these future‑dated records for the affected employment often remain in the system. As a result:

  • HR administrators must manually identify and delete records
  • Operational effort increases during the already time‑sensitive termination processes
  • Lingering records can lead to data inconsistencies, potentially impacting reporting and payroll

For organizations managing complex or high‑volume terminations, this manual cleanup quickly becomes a recurring administrative burden.

The Solution

The new automated deletion feature addresses this challenge directly.

Once configured, SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central automatically deletes future‑dated Job Information and Compensation Information records that fall after the (Job Information) termination date of an employment. The automation applies only to the terminated employment, ensuring that any other active employment for the same person remains unaffected.

The feature works consistently across all termination channels, including:

  • Web UI
  • Mobile
  • APIs
  • Imports

 This ensures reliable behavior regardless of how employment terminations are processed.

How It Works

Administrators can enable the “Delete Future‑Dated Records After Termination” setting for specific event reasons used in their business processes.

New setting in Event Reason Configuration

  • All future‑dated Job Information and Compensation Information records (including recurring pay components) with an event reason for which the setting is enabled are automatically removed
  • The deletion applies only to the terminated employment
  • The system performs the cleanup without any additional manual steps
  • All deletions are captured in audit logs, supporting transparency and compliance requirements

The feature is enabled by default but remains fully under administrative control. System admins can determine which event reasons should result in a deletion.

Example: Terminate an employment on May 1, 2026 

Note: A Termination entered for May 1, 2026 results in a Job Information Termination Date of May 2, 2026 - the first day an employment becomes inactive. 

Data before Termination 

Job Information History with an upcoming promotion effective June 1, 2026

Compensation Information History with an upcoming promotion effective June 1, 2026

 

Data After Termination 

Job Information History after Termination - the future dated promotion was automatically deleted

Compensation Information History after Termination - the future-dated Promotion was automatically deleted

Want to see the feature in action – watch the demo video.

What This Means for You

Less manual cleanup, fewer data inconsistencies, and more time for your HR teams to focus on strategic work. It’s a practical improvement that makes day‑to‑day operations smoother.

Learn More and Take Action

To explore configuration details and include this feature in your implementations or system optimization activities:

  • Review the feature in the What’s New Viewer: Automated Deletion of Future-Dated Job and Compensation Information Records After Employment Termination
  • Visit the original SAP Customer Influence Idea (249640) that inspired this enhancement.
  • Review the implementation guide for this feature: Creating Event Reasons for Employee Central | SAP Help Portal

Join the conversation

Have you started using automated deletion after employment termination, or are you planning to enable it? Share your thoughts, questions, or feedback in the comments below. We’d love to hear from you.

entry.title: From Idea to App in Minutes: Generating SAP Fiori Elements Applications with the Project Accelerator
entry.content:
The SAP Fiori tools Project Accelerator is an AI-assisted capability that transforms business requirements provided as text, image, or a combination of both into a SAP Fiori elements application. The generated application can be backed by either the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) or ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP).

The Project Accelerator automates the generation of data models, services, UI scaffolding, and mock data, enabling you to quickly turn ideas from brainstorming or whiteboarding sessions into tangible applications that can be immediately previewed and iterated upon.

 

Where it fits in the SAP Fiori Tools ecosystem

The Project Accelerator is built directly into SAP Fiori tools and can be used within SAP BAS.

It complements existing SAP Fiori tools capabilities such as project generation, application preview, and annotation editing. By generating initial project artifacts directly from business requirements, the Project Accelerator helps you quickly establish a solid project structure.

 

What the Project Accelerator Generates

Depending on the selected programming model, the Project Accelerator generates the following artifacts:

CAP:

For CAP-based applications, the Project Accelerator generates:

  • An SAP Fiori elements application
  • Data models and services
  • Local mock data

 

RAP :

For RAP-based applications, the Project Accelerator generates:

  • An SAP Fiori elements application
  • A RAP back-end artifact
  • An OData V4 service endpoint
  • Local mock data

 

Important note: Business logic is not automatically generated in either CAP or RAP projects. Developers must implement the application logic manually after the initial scaffolding is created.

 

 

Step-by-step guide for the RAP Accelerator Through Business Application Studio (BAS)

In this guide, we will generate an SAP Fiori elements app for managing sports games using the RAP Accelerator with image input. This app will allow users to view game schedules, team information, and game details. Later, we will modify the application to target soccer.

 

 

 

1) Prepare your input

Before starting the generation process, gather your business requirements, which are the inputs you prove to the Project Accelerator.

The Input can be in the following formats:

 

Text

You can write a clear description of your application, including:

  • The purpose of the app
  • Entities and their relationships
  • Fields and properties
  • Labels and restrictions
  • Desired UI layout

Tip: Field values, logic, and restrictions are usually easier to describe using text.

 

Images

You can upload images to describe the desired structure or layout, such as:

  • A data model diagram
  • A UI mockup
  • A List Report layout showing columns
  • Object Page sections or tables
  • Screenshots of an existing application

Tip: Layouts and visual structures are easier to describe using images.

 

I'll use the following image to build a sports game management scenario. Users will be able to see which teams are playing against each other, the league and age group, and where and when the game takes place. The app will also display team details, including the trainer and players.

 

Figure 1. Database model for sports game management scenario

When I first generated the application without specifying a sport type, the underlying LLM inferred the context and generated soccer-related mock data. To see how changes to the input such as specifying the sport type affects the output, I regenerated the application with several modifications to the original image. The results, shown in the Appendix , demonstrate how the AI adapts the generated data based on more specific input.

 

Using Multimedia Input

It is important to keep in mind that the accelerator accepts a single file as input. If you want to upload both text and images, or multiple images, you should first combine them into a single document, either as a word document file (.docx) or as a markdown (.md) file and later upload the file to the accelerator as the business requirement.

If you are using a .md file, you should also upload the images to your workspace folder.

 

 

2) Set up your environment:

To access the Project Accelerator, you need to be in Dev Space with the necessary extensions installed.

Prerequisites

  1. You need to have access to SAP Build Code (What is SAP Build Code | SAP Help Portal)  or signed in for (SAP Build Code Test Drive | SAP Tutorials)
  2. You need to use SAP Business Application Studio as your integrated development environment

 

Required Extensions

Based on your programming model, ensure your Dev Space includes the appropriate extensions:

(If you are creating a new Dev Space, you can select an application type that includes the extensions you need: creating a new Dev Space.)

 

For CAP:

Dev Space must include:

  • CAP Tools
  • SAP Fiori Tools

 

For RAP

Dev must include:

  • SAP Fiori Tools

 

RAP Backend Requirements:

For RAP projects, ensure the following prerequisites are met:

  • RAP backend should be version 2602 or higher
  • You should be connected to the backend system by configuring the destination in SAP Business Application Studio
  • S_DEVELOP authorization

 

Creating a Dev Space

Now, moving on with the creation of a Dev Space:

  1.  Open SAP Build
    1. Click the icon with small squares on the top-right corner
    2. Select Dev Space ManagerFigure 2. How to access the Dev Space Manager
  2. Create a New Dev Space
    • Enter a name for your Dev Space
    • Select the application type 
      • Each application type includes a predefined set of extensions.
      • Select the application type based on the extensions it provides and whether they are required for your programming model or not.
      • For this example, I selected the Full-Stack ABAP Application.

         

    • Click on “Create Dev Space” Figure 3. How to create a Dev Space
  3. After creating your dev space, you will be redirected to the Dev Space Manager, where you can see the newly created dev space and access any other dev spaces you may have created.Figure 4. Dev Space with status "starting"
  4. Wait until the status changes to "Running"
  5. Click on the Dev Space name to start developing

Image 5. Dev Space with status "running"

 

3)  Launching the Project Accelerator

Once you are in your Dev Space, you can launch the Project Accelerator in multiple ways. One of them being:

Using the Template Wizard

  1. Choose “New Project from Template”Figure 6. Step 1 of launching the Project Accelerator
  2. Select “SAP Fiori Generator" and click "Start"Figure 7 . Step 2 of launching the Project Accelerator
  3. On the next screen, click on the “Project Accelerator” to begin 

Figure 8. Step 3 of launching the Project Accelerator

 

 

4) Choose your programming model accelerator

When using the Project Accelerator, you can either select CAP Accelerator or RAP Accelerator tab depending on your programming model.

 

RAP Configuration

For RAP projects, you must:

  1. Specify the Destination:
    1. Select a destination system from the dropdown
    2. Verify your user authentication (user and password for the backend system)
      • OAuth2 authentication can also be configured via the SAP BTP CockpitFigure 9. Accessing the RAP Accelerator
  2. Configure Backend Connection 

 After successful authentication, additional fields will appear:

Here, you should specify your SAPUI5 repository, the package for RAP backend artifact creation, and the transport request if it is required.

Figure 10. Configuring the backend connection

 

 For more information regarding these fields, please refer to: Generating an Application Using an Image | SAP Help Portal

 

 

5)   Enter Your Business Requirements

After choosing your accelerator and specifying your destination, you can upload the input prepared in Step 1 into the business requirements field.

You can either:

  • Upload files to the workspace and then select them using "Choose File" under the business requirements input area
  • Drag and drop documents directly from the file explorer

Hold the Shift key while dragging

Figure 11. Entering the Business Requirements

 

Note: The clearer and more structured your input is, the better the generated result will be.

 

 

6)   Generating the Project

Note: By default, the application is generated in the currently open folder. If you want the project to be created in a different location, you can specify a Project Folder Path before starting the generation.

  1. Click Generate below the input field:
    • The tool will interpret your requirements and generate the project artifacts.Figure 12. Generating the project
    • The interface switches to a generation view displaying progress. Generation may take some time depending on the complexity of your requirementsFigure 13. Generation view

       

    • Once the generation is complete, the Application Information page will appear.Figure 14. Application Information page

       

    • If the project is not in a workspace folder, BAS may display a dialog asking whether to add it after the generation is complete.Figure 15. Adding the project into a workspace

      In this case, you can choose either:

      • “Open Folder”, which creates a new folder for the project, or
      • “Add Project to Workplace”, adding the project to the existing folder. Selecting one of these options adds the project to the workspace and lands you to the Application Information page.

 

 

7)   Preview of the Application

To preview the application:

  1. Select “Preview Application”Figure 16. Previewing the application
  2. Choose “Start Fiori Run” from the context menu
  3. This launches the generated UI.Figure 17. Preview of the application

     

Note: At this stage, the preview only shows the application structure, without any data.

 

Edge Case: Running the Preview from the ABAP Backend

Make sure that you run the preview directly from SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) when previewing the generated application.

If you launch the preview from the ABAP backend instead of BAS, the UI will not render correctly. This is expected behavior as the Project Accelerator stores UI annotations in the local “annotation.xm” file of your Fiori project, not in backend Metadata Extensions. Therefore, the backend cannot render the UI properly without these local annotations.

If the preview is executed from the ABAP backend, the result will appear similar to the following:

Figure 18. Preview of the application when rendered from the ABAP backend example 1

Figure 19. Preview of the application when rendered from the ABAP backend example 2

 

 

8 ) Generate and Preview with Local Mock Data

You can also preview the application with locally generated mock data:

  1. Open the terminal
  2. Enter Ctrl + C to terminate the current process
  3. Select “Preview Application”
  4. Choose “Start-Mock Fiori Run” from the context menuFigure 20. Previewing the application with locally generated mock data
  5. After restarting, you may need to log in again for system authorization.
  6. The UI will now display generated mock data, allowing you to:

Figure 21. Preview of the application with mock dataFigure 22. Preview of the application with mock data - game object page

Figure 23. Preview of the application with mock data - team object page

 

 

9)   Review and Refine

After generation, review the application and refine the configuration as needed. You can adjust field names, labels, page sections, and UI layout using the Page Map:

  1. Click on "Open Page Map"Figure 24. Navigating to Page Map 
  2. Select the pencil icon next to the location you want to modify 
  3. Make your changes.
  4. The preview will automatically refresh and update

 

Example 1: Editing Column Names

Since the business requirement was for general sports, and soccer uses fields (not courts), let's change the column name from "court" to "field."

  • Scenario: Change the field name “Home Court” to “Home Field”
  1. Click on Open Page Map
  2. Click on the pencil icon of “Teams Object Page”Figure 25. Navigating to "Teams" Object Page
  3. Expand the “Table” section
  4. Expand the “Columns” subsection
  5. Click on the “Home Court” field
  6. On the right-hand side (RHS), find the “Label” field and change it to “Home Field”Figure 26. How to change the field name
  7. The preview will automatically refresh and update

Figure 27. Changing the field name in "Team" Object Page

 

Note: Renaming the column in the Teams Object Page only changes how it appears on the Teams Object Page. The “Home Court” column on the Games Object Page won’t update automatically:

Figure 28. Display of unchanged field name in "Game" Object Page

To change it on the Games Object Page as well, you’ll need to rename the column in the Game Object Page. You can do this just by repeating the steps above for Games Object Page.

 

Example 2: Adding a Description to an Object Page

  •  Scenario: Add the description “Game Information” to the Game Object Page.Figure 29. Empty description space in "Game" Object Page
  1. Click on Open Page Map
  2. Select “Games Object Page”Figure 30. Navigating to "Game" Object Page
  3. Click on “Header”.
  4. On the RHS, find Description Type and select “Text” from the dropdown.Figure 31. Navigating to Description Type dropdown

Choosing “Text” allows you to enter custom free text. Alternatively, you can select “Property” to pick from predefined fields or objects.

 In this case, I selected the “Text” option because “Game Information” is not a predefined feature of any existing object in the application.

5. Type “Game Information” into the Description field.

Figure 32. How to add a text to the description space

6. The UI will automatically refresh and update

Figure 33. Preview of "Game" Object Page with description text

 

Example 3: Adding an Icon to an Object Page

  • Scenario: Adding a soccer icon to the Game object page
  1. Click on Open Page Map
  2. Select the “Games Object Page”
  3. Find the icon sectionFigure 34. Navigating to the Icon Explorer
  4. Click the link to the Icon Explorer
  5. Search for “soccer”Image 35. View of the Icon Explorer and searching for the term "soccer"You can also browse the available icons and select the one that fits your goalsFigure 36. Overview of the existing SAPUI5-supported icons
  6. Copy the soccer ball icon’s URL

Figure 37. Copying the soccer ball icon's URL

7. Switch to your Dev Space and paste the URL into the icon fieldFigure 38. Pasting the copied URL into the Icon field8. The UI will refresh automatically and display the iconFigure 39. Preview of the "Game" Object Page with the soccer ball icon

 

Next steps:

After you are satisfied with the UI Layout;

  • For CAP: Add business logic in service handlers; enrich annotations; refine data model.
  • RAP: Implement behavior logic, validation, determinations; adjust annotations; refine UI.

For more information:

  • Official documentation page:

Generating an Application with the Project Accelerator Using SAP Fiori Tools AI | SAP Help Portal

 

Appendix

1)    Specifying the sport type as volleyball


 To see how specifying the sport type affects the generated mock data, I added additional requirements database model I originally used:Figure 40. Updated database model, sport type specified as volleyball

The generated mock data became more volleyball-oriented, as reflected in the “Team” and “Place” names:

Figure 41. Preview with volleyball-specific mock data

I was also interested to see if the number of players would change based on the specified sport type, for example:

  • a volleyball team having at least 6 players
  • a soccer team having 11
  • a basketball team having 5

However, the number of players did not change compared to the original input.

Figure 42. "Team" Object Page displaying players

 If you want to have specific number of instances, you can explicitly include this in your business requirements:Figure 43. Data model diagram with amount of team members specified

Figure 44. "Team" Object Page with 6 players

 

2)    Using Mock UI

For the Mock UI, I uploaded an image intended to represent the list of all games and the corresponding object page:

Figure 45. Mock UI - First attempt

However, the LLM did not recognize this image as one of its supported input types and returned an error:

If you are providing a Mock UI, the input should be clear and focused to avoid such errors.

Figure 46. Error message for unsupported images

In my experience, Mock UIs produce the best results when the input is as simple and clear as possible. For example, the image below worked well:

Figure 47. Mock UI - Second attempt

The Project Accelerator successfully generated the list report and even added “Players” and “Teams” objects to the object page, which was a positive surprise:

Figure 48. Successfully generated application with Mock UIImage 49. Successfully generated application with Mock UI - Object Page

 

3)    Using Multimedia Input: Multiple images

I combined the input material into a single .docx document and uploaded it to the accelerator:Figure 50. The two images, combined in a single document

You can upload the .docx or .md files the same way as you would for images.

The following application was generated:Figure 51. Preview of the application generated with multi-media input

Figure 52. Preview of the application generated with multi-media input - Object Page

 

Mismatch in object fields  

When combining multiple input sources, such as database tables and Mock UI images, it is important that these models are consistent. Inconsistencies between the data model and the UI definition can lead to incorrect or incomplete application generation:

Figure 53. Multi-media input with mismatching fields

Figure 54. Preview of the application generated with the input shown in Figure 53

 The following issue occurred due to such a mismatch:

  • The “Game” entity contains additional fields in the database model that are not represented in the UI.
  • The “Team” entity is missing a role field in the database model that is expected by the UI.

entry.title: SAP Revenue Growth Management Integrated with SAP BDC: Seamless Data Integration & Governed Insights
entry.content:
1. Why CPG Organizations Struggle with Fragmented Data and Slow Insights 

Key account managers (KAMs), IT admins, and partners in the Consumer Products industry all wrestle with the same trio of issues: fragmented data across ERP, CRM, and market sources; long and fragile integrations for each new initiative; and analytics that take too long to trust, let alone act on.

The result is slower promotion cycles, delayed deductions and trade claims reconciliation, and AI projects that stall because the “single source of truth” never quite materializes. SAP Business Data Cloud (SAP BDC) exists to break that cycle—and when SAP Revenue Growth Management (RGM) integrates as part of the Consumer Products Intelligence solution on SAP BDC, the platform turns integration projects into a guided setup and makes governed, AI‑ready data the default state.

 

2. Key Concepts Explained: SAP BDC, Data Products & Intelligent Apps 

SAP Business Data Cloud (SAP BDC) is a fully managed SaaS solution that unifies and governs all SAP data and seamlessly connects with third-party data—enabling data management, analytics, and AI capabilities. It brings together the existing capabilities of SAP Datasphere, SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP Business Warehouse (BW) and incorporates SAP Databricks, line of business data products, and SAP’s open data ecosystem partners to create an integrated solution. 

Data Products (DPs) are curated, reusable datasets—packaged with metadata and APIs—so applications can consume or produce data without point‑to‑point integration. In this model, a producer is any application that creates and publishes data products into SAP BDC, while a consumer is any application or tool that reads those data products from SAP BDC to support analytics, AI, or operational processes. 

SAP Business Data Cloud Intelligent Applications are SAP‑managed apps built on certified data products that deliver embedded analytics and AI‑powered experiences out of the box.  

 

3. How it All Connects: Architecture Overview 

 

The diagram above illustrates the Consumer Products Intelligence solution in SAP Business Data Cloud—which includes SAP Revenue Growth Management. 

A CPG manufacturer uses SAP Revenue Growth Management to manage trade promotions. When a KAM creates a promotion within the application, SAP Revenue Growth Management consumes S/4HANA master data and sales- and finance-related data products via SAP BDC to power its core features, including promotion planning and embedded analytics. 

Once the promotion is approved and released, SAP Revenue Growth Management writes promotion data back to S/4HANA—supporting processes such as Claims and Condition Contract Management in SAP Revenue Growth Management. 

At the same time, SAP Revenue Growth Management produces its own data products back into SAP BDC, making them available to SAP Analytics Cloud. These dashboards help KAMs quickly view spend vs. uplift and spot which accounts in their territory delivered the strongest promotion ROI—so those winning promotions can be copied to drive future revenue growth. 

 

4. How SAP Revenue Growth Management on SAP BDC Delivers Value 

4.1. Automated and Seamless Integration Across SAP Systems 

Instead of wiring SAP Revenue Growth Management to each source system, users add the solution to an SAP BDC “formation” (a logical grouping of your SAP systems) in SAP for Me. The platform then handles the essential integration steps and installs the required data products for users—reducing manual setup and ambiguity. For example, SAP Revenue Growth Management automatically receives the S/4HANA data products it needs without manual setup.  

 

4.2. Harmonized SAP Data: Powered by Data Products and One Domain Model (ODM) 

SAP Revenue Growth Management's embedded analytics feature—five dashboards covering promotion performance, revenue, spend vs. uplift, volume, and profit and loss—has always been built into the application. With the SAP BDC integration, the underlying data powering these dashboards now flows through standardized, governed data products rather than point-to-point connections. Because all SAP data is exposed as standardized data products following SAP's One Domain Model (ODM), data across different SAP systems is harmonized by design—making it effortless to combine and analyze across systems. This lays the foundation for increasingly powerful analytics capabilities going forward, including customizable dashboards and broader cross‑system analysis. 

  

4.3. Extensibility: Unlocking Third-Party Data for a More Complete Picture 

The integration of SAP Revenue Growth Management with SAP BDC also opens the door for customers to enrich their analysis with data from outside the SAP ecosystem. As SAP BDC continues to evolve, customers will be able to import their own third-party data into SAP BDC in a standardized way, enabling it to flow into SAP Revenue Growth Management without custom pipelines or one-off integrations. 

For example, a beverage company will be able to import sell-out data—such as point-of-sale records from retailers showing actual consumer purchases—and combine it with promotion and account plan data directly within SAP Revenue Growth Management for scenario-based analysis. The result: a more complete picture of promotion effectiveness, grounded in both SAP data and real-world market signals. 

 

5. What’s New: SAP Revenue Growth Management on SAP BDC 

5.1. Streamlined Data Product Consumption Through End-to-End Provisioning Automation 

Guided Wizard in SAP for Me

Previously, provisioning required separate manual steps—creating a formation, configuring connectivity, and installing data products individually. Now, as an intelligent application, the Consumer Products Intelligence solution in SAP Business Data Cloud provides a guided wizard in SAP for Me that handles tenant creation, SAP BDC integration setup, and connectivity configuration—without IT admins needing to interpret long setup guides. 

 

Automatic Dependency Management

SAP Revenue Growth Management automatically consumes the SAP‑managed data products it depends on. IT admins and onboarding partners no longer need to do any manual work—all relevant dependencies are automatically identified, installed, and activated during the provisioning process. 

 

5.2. Standardized Data Product Production for Richer Insights 

Previously, accessing application data required custom API integrations and one-time development effort for each connection—costly to build and difficult to maintain. Now, as SAP Revenue Growth Management processes key business data such as promotion plans, baselines, actuals, and analytics, it produces its own standardized data products into SAP BDC. These data products share a harmonized data model with other SAP data products, enabling customers to conduct holistic analysis across their data landscape—and empowering agentic AI capabilities in Joule to deliver insightful suggestions and actions grounded in rich, connected business data. 

 

6. Join Our Co-Innovation Initiative 

The integration ofSAP Revenue Growth Management with SAP Business Data Cloud marks a significant step toward faster setup, governed analytics, and connected insights for Consumer Products companies. 

By unifying inbound consumption of S/4HANA data products and outbound data products from SAP Revenue Growth Management for analytics and extension, we aim to help CPG companies stay agile and competitive in a rapidly changing market. 

If you’re an existing or prospective customer willing to explore these capabilities firsthand, you're welcome to join our co‑innovation program. Contact us at RGM_Engage@sap.com to start the journey toward modernizing trade promotion execution and unlocking governed insights across commercial processes. 

 

More Information

Product Page for SAP Revenue Growth Management 

Documentation on SAP Help Portal

Topic Page for SAP Revenue Growth Management

    • Introducing Calendar View in SAP Revenue Growth Management: A Visual Leap for Strategic Planning
    • Decoding Promotional Success: Embedded Analytics in SAP Revenue Growth Management

entry.title: Re: Error while closing Asset Fiscal Year where Fiscal ye...
entry.content:
If you left 2024 open in Asset Accounting, then all assets in the company code can potentially have their depreciation changed if you make any changes to the assets, then all open years in Asset Accounting will be recalculated to take into account any changes made. 

Review the assets to see what changed, reverse and undo the changes to see if the outstanding planned depreciation can be undone.

entry.title: MuleSoft vs SAP Integration Suite: Which One Fits Your Enterprise?
entry.content:
STRATEGY PAPER - INTEGRATION PLATFORM ASSESSMENT

SAP Integration Suite Vs MuleSoft Anypoint Platform

Choosing an integration platform is one of the most consequential infrastructure decisions an enterprise can make. Both SAP Integration Suite and MuleSoft Anypoint Platform are mature, enterprise-grade solutions. This paper offers an objective, fact-based view of where SAP Integration Suite delivers a distinct strategic advantage for organisations whose landscape is built on SAP.

 

1. Setting the Context

While MuleSoft Anypoint Platform has long maintained a strong presence in the enterprise integration space, SAP Integration Suite, a core component of the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) has gained significant traction among organizations pursuing cloud-first and SAP-centric strategies.

Many enterprises are reassessing their reliance on MuleSoft as part of broader integration modernization initiatives. Factors such as rising total cost of ownership, challenges in optimizing integrations within SAP-heavy landscapes, and increasing demand for faster, scalable innovation are contributing to this shift.

As a result, SAP Integration Suite is increasingly being considered as a strategic alternative for organizations seeking tighter SAP alignment, improved cost efficiency, and enhanced agility in their integration landscape

The key question is not which platform is 'better', it is which platform is the right fit for your organisation's ecosystem, skill base, and strategic direction. For businesses running SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Ariba, or other SAP cloud solutions, the answer increasingly points to SAP Integration Suite.

 

2. Head-to-Head: Feature Comparison

The table below summarises verified, up-to-date comparisons across key integration criteria. 

 

Criteria

MuleSoft Anypoint Platform

SAP Integration Suite

Development effort

Higher complexity - Anypoint Studio (Eclipse-based IDE), Java/DataWeave expertise required. Steeper onboarding for new teams.

Lower effort - Browser-based graphical iFlow design. Pre-built SAP content packages accessible to developers and consultants. Low code, no code platform.

Message mapping

DataWeave used for mapping is proprietary and complex.

Graphical drag-and-drop mapping editor with built-in functions. Groovy/XSLT available for advanced cases. Easier to build and maintain.

Libraries

Need to import various Java libraries to develop integrations.

Most of the libraries are handled at the product level.

Event-driven architecture

No native built in event broker. External tools (RabbitMQ, Kafka, ActiveMQ) required, adding infrastructure cost and operational overhead.

SAP Event Mesh and Advanced Event Mesh is a fully managed native capability on BTP. Supports pub/sub, event streaming, multi-cloud distribution out of the box.

Transport & deployment

Changes to one flow require full application redeployment, less flexibility, risk involved and longer release cycles.

Artifact-level transport via Cloud Transport Management Service. Move individual iFlows independently across landscapes. Faster, safer releases.

SAP ecosystem connectivity

Third-party connectors or custom BAPI/RFC adapters needed for SAP systems. Additional build and maintenance effort.

Native connectors and thousands of pre-built packages on SAP Business Accelerator Hub. Zero custom work for core SAP integrations.

Custom adapter development

500+ connectors on Anypoint Exchange. SDK makes building custom connectors for non-SAP systems straightforward.

Strength in SAP-centric environment. Non-SAP custom adapters require deeper technical expertise.

Cost model

Premium vCore-based licensing. Costs can escalate unpredictably with integration volume growth.

BTP consumption-based model. Organisations with existing BTP entitlements get significant IS capacity included.

Monitoring & operations

Anypoint Monitoring provides dashboards and alerts. Comprehensive observability often requires custom configuration.

Simple Message monitoring, Alert Notification Service, Cloud ALM - all standard provided by out of the box by SAP. No additional tooling required.

Retry / error handling

Custom coding required (Until Successful scope or custom error flows). Risk of inconsistency across flows.

Built-in JMS-based retry with dead-letter queue handling. Persistent messaging, configurable without custom code.

Downtime handling

While MuleSoft CloudHub 2.0 offers zero-downtime via Rolling Updates and Blue-Green deployment, it requires specific replica configurations and vCore infrastructure overhead.

SAP Integration Suite provides seamless artifact deployment natively without additional infrastructure management.

Deployment Speed

MuleSoft deployments typically range from 3–10 minutes due to runtime initialization.

SAP Integration Suite typically completes in 10–45 seconds by updating the artifact in memory.

Salesforce Pub/Sub API(gRPC over HTTP/2)

While MuleSoft supports gRPC Connector with underlying high-performance API technology it often requires granular configuration, custom code or flow design to implement the same pub/sub streaming logic.

SAP Integration Suite provides a prebuilt Pub/Sub adapter that abstracts gRPC complexity, enabling configuration-driven consumption of Salesforce events without requiring protocol-level implementation.

 

MuleSoft offers flexibility at the cost of increased development complexity.

SAP Integration Suite reduces implementation effort and accelerates time-to-value

 

3. SAP Integration Suite — Key Strategic Differentiators

For organisations running SAP at their core, the following capabilities represent genuinely differentiated value that MuleSoft cannot replicate without additional investment.

 

BTP consumption pricing

Pay for what you use. Existing BTP entitlements often include significant Integration Suite capacity.

Advanced Event Mesh

Built-in event broker on BTP. No external Kafka/RabbitMQ needed. Real-time event streaming natively.

Artifact-level transport

UI driven, native transport mechanism to move individual iFlows across Dev → QA → Prod. No full application redeployment required.

 

Built-in monitoring

Message monitoring, Alert Notification Service, and Cloud ALM standard out of the box.

Business Accelerator Hub

Thousands of pre-built integration packages from SAP and partners. Reduces build effort significantly.

 

4. When Should Organisations Consider SAP Integration Suite?

Migration decisions should never be taken lightly. The following signals suggest a closer evaluation of SAP Integration Suite is warranted:

  • SAP-first digital core: Your organisation has standardised on S/4HANA and SAP cloud solutions. The majority of integration flows touch SAP systems.
  • MuleSoft licensing costs: Integration volumes are scaling and MuleSoft vCore costs are growing faster than the business value they deliver.
  • Legacy integration retirement: Moving off SAP PI/PO or MuleSoft - SAP Integration Suite is the natural destination for organisations consolidating on BTP.
  • Event-driven ambitions without broker overhead: If real-time event-driven patterns are on the roadmap, Event Mesh removes the need for external broker infrastructure.
  • Low integration cost: SAP Integration Suite is a low code platform requiring less development effort overall.

7. SAP Integration Suite & Salesforce — A Closer Look

Salesforce is the world's leading CRM platform. SAP is the world's leading ERP platform. Making them work together seamlessly is one of the most common, and most strategically important integration challenges enterprises face. This section gives an honest, balanced view of how SAP Integration Suite handles Salesforce integration.

An important nuance: MuleSoft is owned by Salesforce, giving it a structural first-party advantage in the Salesforce ecosystem - early API access, deep product alignment, and a large connector community. SAP Integration Suite wins on a different dimension listed above and primarily on SAP side of integration, where it has unmatched native depth.

SAP Integration Suite "Salesforce + SAP S4/HANA integration"

use cases

Use case

What it does

SAP IS advantage

Quote-to-cash

Salesforce opportunity or quote triggers SAP S4/HANA sales order creation automatically

Pre-built iFlow on SAP API Business Accelerator Hub. Native S/4HANA OData APIs used directly - no custom mapping layer needed

Customer master sync

Bidirectional sync of customer/account records between Salesforce and SAP

Handles complex SAP customer master data model models. Pre-built content manages mapping and prevents duplicate creation.

Order status updates

SAP fulfilment and delivery status pushed back to Salesforce in near-real-time

Event-driven via SAP Advanced Event Mesh. Sales reps see live order status without leaving CRM

Invoice and payment sync

SAP-generated invoices and payment status reflected in Salesforce.

Directly surfaces SAP FI/CO(Financial) data to Salesforce, ensuring accurate revenue tracking.

Real-time credit checks

Sales reps query SAP credit exposure directly from within Salesforce before quoting

On-demand SAP data pull via OData API, orchestrated by IS iFlow. Result returned to Salesforce in seconds

Event-driven order events

Salesforce Pub/Sub events consumed by SAP IS to trigger downstream SAP processes

Native Salesforce Pub/Sub adapter - real-time, no polling, no external broker required

Conclusion

For SAP-centric enterprises, SAP Integration Suite is not simply 'good enough' - it is increasingly the superior strategic choice. Key reasons (including for Salesforce integration):

•  Lower total cost of ownership when BTP entitlements are already in place.

•  Native connectivity eliminates custom adapter work for SAP applications.

•  Built-in Event Mesh removes the need for external broker infrastructure.

•  Artifact - level transport enables safer, faster release cycles.

•  Pre-packaged integration content from the Business Accelerator Hub accelerates delivery.

•  Better developer productivity due to out of box functionality by the platform.

•  Browser-based tooling integrated within the SAP ecosystem, enabling centralized development and management.

“This is an independent analysis based on project-based experience and publicly available information”

entry.title: Re: RBP Permissions have been affected with the new peopl...
entry.content:
Please go through the Link - What You Need to Do After Migration | SAP Help Portal

Step 4: Manage access control for Full Profile categories

The User Permissions  - Employee Views - Employee Views Section permissions that allow users to access legacy People Profile sections are no longer supported for Full Profile. Starting with 1H 2026 release, permission items for categories in Full Profile are available in the User Permissions -  Employee Views - People Profile Categories permission list. Use RBP reports as a reference when you configure permissions for Full Profile categories.

If you have already selected the RBPs' before the 1H Release for each of the People Profile Categories, then you would see all of them under User Permissions -  Employee Views - People Profile Categories permission list. If you have not configured earlier then in that case you have download the Employee Views Permission Report and based on the RBPs' listed in each of the categories, individually go to each of the permission role and select the checkbox under People Profile Categories. If there are any of the Categories which are deleted and the card inside that category is moved to other category, than the deleted category won't show up in the People Profile categories list however it will be shown under Employee View Sections (only for Legacy People Profile).

entry.title: Trouble getting On-Prem / Private Cloud quotes for new customer – anyone else?
entry.content:
Hey all,

We’ve recently run into an issue as an SAP partner while trying to get licensing quotes for a new customer (net new to SAP).

We initiated the DDA process, but it ended up being denied, and the only option we were able to move forward with was Public Cloud.

The challenge is that the client is in the financial sector, with pretty strict regulatory requirements, and they already have a lot of systems hosted in their own data center that the ERP needs to integrate with. Because of that, on-prem would clearly be the best fit, with private cloud as a second option.

At the moment, though, it feels like those options are basically off the table.

So I’m curious — what are your recent experiences with getting quotes for on-prem or private cloud for new customers? Have you managed to get them approved, and if so, how?

Is there any realistic way to escalate this internally, or is Public Cloud essentially the only path forward now given SAP’s direction?

Would appreciate any insights

entry.title: Re: Manage Project Billing Requests - Approver does not g...
entry.content:
Hello @AndrewDain 

Thank you for your question.

The reason for that is that Approver is a workflow related information, while app’s export function exports the selected billing elements/billing request data to an XLSX file.

Consider using My Inbox-Project Billing request app for approval processing and approver related workflow information, not the Excel export of Manage Project Billing Requests.

So, since you can add an Approver to the results grid, but it does not appear in the export file, that would mean, the column is available in the UI for display, but it is not part of the export data set the app delivers.

3453820 - Information tab for Project Billing Requests workflow task is empty in the 'My inbox - All items' app - SAP for Me

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/29cf986299714386847f4d4c9bb86994/09bddf5612e54600a06539c2e4250d30.html?locale=en-US

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Simplifying API Authentication with External OAuth in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Managing authentication across multiple identity providers can get complex—especially when partners bring their own identity systems.
With External OAuth (OIDC) in SAP Integration Suite, you can now validate and propagate tokens from providers like Microsoft Entra, Google, or Okta—enabling secure, seamless, and standardized authentication across systems.

This means:
· Trust external identity providers
· Reduce dependency on a single auth mechanism (like XSUAA)
· Enable smooth partner onboarding
· Simplify cross-platform API security

Learn more:

  • Blog post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/secure-apis-external-oidc-based-authentic…
  • Help guide: https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/authentication-fa6eec4f9ffc45aa89…

entry.title: Background Job For Processing of Invoices
entry.content:
Is it possible to schedule the background processing (release) of "To Be Posted" billing documents in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud using dedicated Fiori apps? Can it be set up in such a way that a report is generated once the job is complete?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Background Job For Processing of Invoices
entry.content:
To process documents that are "To Be Posted" (meaning they need to be released to financial accounting), you can use the Schedule Billing Release app.

This app allows you to schedule the release of billing documents to financial accounting, which triggers the creation of journal entries. You can set up both single and recurring background jobs.

You can filter which documents to process based on various criteria, including date functions.

While a traditional standalone "report" isn't automatically emailed as a document, the system provides a comprehensive job log for verification. After the background job runs, you can use the built-in job log to check if the release was successful or to diagnose any specific issues/errors.

entry.title: Negative Stock in Transit (MB5T) after STO Reversal – Unable to Reverse GRN (Previous Year Posting)
entry.content:
Negative Stock in Transit (MB5T) after STO Reversal – Unable to Reverse GRN (Previous Year Posting)

Issue Description:
The issue occurred in an intercompany STO scenario with the following process flow:

  1. STO PO was created.
  2. Outbound Delivery was created and PGI was completed.
  3. The receiving plant performed GRN (Movement type 101) with reference to the outbound delivery.
  4. Subsequently, the sending plant reversed Billing, PGI, and the Outbound Delivery.

Current Situation:

  • MB5T is showing negative stock in transit for the receiving plant.
  • There is an inconsistency between issuing and receiving plant stock.
  • GRN reversal is not possible as the transaction was posted in the previous financial year and the posting period is now closed.

Could you please suggest the best possible way to clear the negative stock in transit in MB5T in this scenario?

  • Is there any standard SAP approach to correct this mismatch without reversing historical GRN?

entry.title: Re: app " Schedule Tax Jobs" public cloud
entry.content:
Good day @pietroidone 

Thank you for your question.

The limitations you see is based on the Public Cloud’s design, not a missing configuration. Here is the reason.

  • The Advance Return for Tax job template in Schedule Tax Jobs is the template you should use to create the advance return and, where relevant, also handle the document numbering update logic.
  • The Additional List for Advance Return for Tax on Sales/Purchase is a supporting report and not the main advance return processing job.

See the following references for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/452cbbca00ca4a79aa2e2a5171d5e283.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/doc/a9616f4a29354fc297c58499b754e3f8/2022/en-US/loio4857c0540cf5ef05e10000000a4450e5.pdf

So, no, the schedule job template does not expose the same selections that are available in the interactive app, and the Additional List job is not the correct substitute when you need to update document numbering.

For your exact requirement to exclude certain document types via Dynamic Selections, the Public Cloud does not currently expose it in the standard job template, so, don’t assume it is supported in scheduled execution.

The options you have in Public Cloud is for you to:

  • Run the main Advance Return for Tax job in Schedule Tax Jobs when you need document numbering update.
  • Use the interactive app when you specifically need flexibility in selection such as Dynamic Selections.
  • The gap between the two is more of a product limitation rather than a configuration issue.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Error while closing Asset Fiscal Year where Fiscal ye...

We will try to undo the changes made for Leased Asset no. 1 in FY 2025 (original Lease Term was reduced here), and we will check if the planned due amount in EUR vanishes.

For Lease Asset No. 2 :- scenario is as follows:

Leased Asset was Capitalized in fiscal period 07/2021, but depreciation start date was selected from fiscal period 01/2022. So the depreciation for initial 06 periods for FY2021 was posted as unplanned depreciation in period 01/2022, amount 2970.38 EUR.

Shrinivas

 

Shrinivas

entry.title: New Partner Content on SAP Business Accelerator Hub (Q1 ’26)
entry.content:
In continuation of our previous blog, we are excited to share the latest updates and continue delivering valuable insights to our community.

In this edition of our blog series, we highlight the newest content packages (particularly APIs and Integrations) released on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub by our partners in Q1 2026, along with high-level details.

For complete details on each partner's content, see the partners listing on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub Home page.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Avalara

Integration packages published:

SAP S/4HANA Integration with Avalara Avatax Sales and Use Tax

Sales and use tax rates are varied and complex across counties, states, and countries. And because the burden of remaining compliant is on the business, there’s a big financial risk to getting it wrong. Even when manual tax processes are manageable, keeping up with the changes can be impossible — there’s always the possibility of legislative regulations, new tax rules, and shifts in product taxability. Rather than spending time ensuring your rates, product taxability, and collection requirements haven’t changed, let our cloud-based SaaS system evaluate each transaction based on the latest laws and rules, without additional effort from your IT or finance teams.

SAP S/4HANA Integration with Avalara Solution Suite

Processes such as routing, authentication and logging that are reused across different integrations have been brought together in a single package.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Incture Technologies Private Limited

Integration packages published:

Microsoft Power BI Integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

The integration of Microsoft Power BI with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition provides a robust and scalable architecture for advanced analytics and business intelligence. This setup leverages SAP Cloud Integration as a secure and flexible middleware to extract, transform, and deliver SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition data to Microsoft Power BI, enabling real-time and insightful data visualization.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition with Shopify

Integration between SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition with Shopify connects Shopify’s e-commerce storefront with SAP’s ERP core. Shopify manages online sales and customer experience, while SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition handles order processing, inventory, pricing, fulfillment, and financials. Data is synchronized in near real time using SAP Cloud Integration.

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll To Banamex ACH

The integration between SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP) and Banamex ACH enables secure and automated transmission of payroll data from SAP’s cloud-based payroll system to the bank for salary disbursement. The solution ensures accurate, compliant, and timely processing of employee payments.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Figaf ApS

Integration package published:

Figaf Integration with ServiceNow

The "Figaf Integration with ServiceNow" package provides a template for integrating Figaf and SNow. It includes highly configurable iFlows processing Figaf webhooks for ticket/transport events to SNow actions, update Figaf based on SNow changes, and a utility for searching tasks.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

QuantityWare GmbH

Integration package published:

QuantityWare Integration with Third Party

The integration between QuantityWare Bulk Calculations Solution (BCS) and non-SAP environments through SAP BTP, provides customers the ability to access BCS from any application or external interface. This expands QuantityWare BCS usage beyond the SAP environment, allowing greater flexibility and integration potential.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPS Commerce Inc

Integration package published:

SPS Commerce Supplier Integration with SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud 

The SPS Commerce Fulfillment Connector for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud enables businesses to effortlessly automate and optimize electronic data interchange (EDI) with trading partners, streamlining order-to-cash processes and enhancing supply chain visibility. This cloud-based solution offers easy configuration, scalable performance, and a single connection to thousands of retailers, vendors, and logistics providers, reducing manual work and errors while supporting business growth. Integrated directly with SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud and the SAP Business Technology Platform through SAP Integration Suite, it provides real-time monitoring, compliance with trading partner requirements, and 24/7 expert support. By leveraging this connector, companies can consolidate orders across channels, achieve seamless data exchange, and maintain a clean core SAP environment.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

PriceFx AG

Integration packages published:

SAP S/4HANA Pricing Procedure Mapping with Pricefx

This integration package contains sample mappings required to replicate pricing procedures from SAP S/4HANA to Pricefx.

SAP S/4HANA Pricing Procedure Integration with Pricefx

This integration package contains integration flows to replicate pricing procedures from SAP S/4HANA to Pricefx. They can be used as templates to speed up your integration between SAP S/4HANA and Pricefx. You could modify message mappings and value mappings according to your needs.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SirionLabs Pte Ltd

Integration packages published:

Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management Integration with SAP Ariba Supplier Management

The Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) Integration with SAP Ariba Supplier Management enables seamless synchronization of supplier between SAP Ariba and Sirion CLM, ensuring a unified and accurate supplier lifecycle across systems.

Supplier requests approved in SAP Ariba triggers the creation of counterparty/supplier in Sirion CLM along with workflow movement ensuring complete synchronization of supplier's information.

Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management Integration with SAP Ariba Contract Workspace

The Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) Contract Workspace Adapter for SAP Ariba seamlessly integrates the contracting flow between the two systems. Contract Requests (CDRs) signed in Sirion CLM are automatically transitioned into Contract Workspaces (CWs) in SAP Ariba along with the associated contract documents, enabling synchronization between the two systems throughout the contracting flow.​

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

RealCore Group GmbH

Integration packages published:

SAP LeanIX Integration with SAP API Management

This integration package enables data synchronization between SAP API Management and SAP LeanIX factsheets by extracting relevant API proxies and provider information and transferring them to SAP LeanIX applications.

SAP Cloud ALM and Jira Task Synchronization

This integration package enables comprehensive and bidirectional synchronization of tasks between SAP Cloud ALM and Atlassian Jira, ensuring transparency and alignment across both platforms. It facilitates seamless collaboration between project and development teams by synchronizing all relevant task data in real time.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

KaTe GmbH

Integration package published:

KaTe Confluent Parallel Consumer Adapter

KaTe confluent parallel consumer adapter allows you to scale on record key level or even process records without order if required.

The adapter implements confluent parallel consumer lib to scale on different configurable metrics as single or batch processing option for high volume processing.

The adapter inherits all features of the KaTe Kafka adapter like support for Avro and Protobuf schemas, schema registries as well as sophisticated error handling and monitoring capabilities built into the SAP integrations suite environment.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Thank you very much for your attention, and we look forward to sharing more exciting content in the future.

If you are interested in Partnering with SAP Business Accelerator Hub, please refer to this section for more details.

@khanhvt4 

Upgrading SAPUI5 to 1.130 or later resolves the issue. Since 1.130 itself is not an LTS release, I recommend upgrading to the next LTS version, 1.136, if you need long-term support.

entry.title: From SAP Build Apps to SAP Build: Rethinking Low-Code Development in the SAP Ecosystem
entry.content:
SAP Build Apps has its roots in SAP’s acquisition of AppGyver, a powerful no-code platform that enabled rapid application development without traditional coding.

What SAP Build Apps Enabled

  • Drag-and-drop UI creation – This is much appreciated by the business
  • Visual logic flows – Good to visualize compared to syntax of the code
  • Mobile-first app design – It matters because 60% of the traffic is from mobile devices
  • Citizen developer empowerment – You have an idea – Voila – then start building!

It played a key role in:

  • Democratizing application development as the business users can build if it is a simple use case
  • Accelerating prototyping – Accessibility accelerated prototyping and check
  • Enabling business-led innovation – democratizing the application development & prototype acceleration enabled the innovation that can be business led.

SAP Build Apps followed a flat architecture, where UI and logic were tightly coupled, making it ideal for simple applications but limiting for enterprise-scale solutions. In the current scenario of ecosystem, while simplicity to build the apps helps but it hinders scale or prevents from building complex enterprise-scale solutions.

And here comes the ‘sunset of SAP Build Apps’ as a standalone strategic tool and it is not a step backward — it is a strategic evolution.

Key Drivers Behind the Transition

  1. From UI-Centric to Process-Centric Development

Organizations today require:

  • Workflow-driven applications rather than UI centric. The UI should be really in alignment with the workflow.
  • Automation-first solutions

SAP Build Apps was primarily: UI-focused, not process-driven

  1. Need for Workflow Orchestration

Modern enterprise apps demand:

  • Approval workflows – If processes are waiting for approvals, then the delay/wait time is inducing lag in creation of value to the business.
  • Task routing – We do not need manual intervention to route the tasks the orchestration should happen automatic for non-critical routing of tasks.
  • Process monitoring – Command center set up has become important for the businesses so that process monitoring can be done to understand the non-value adding sub-processes.

These capabilities require a dedicated process layer, which was not native to SAP Build Apps.

  1. Integration as a First-Class Requirement

Applications must seamlessly connect with:

  • SAP S/4HANA
  • APIs
  • External ecosystems

SAP Build Apps had limited enterprise integration capabilities.

  1. Unified Development Experience

SAP’s strategic direction is to unify:

  • Low-code
  • Pro-code
  • Automation
  • AI

into a single platform.

Enter SAP Build: The Unified Platform

SAP Build represents SAP’s next-generation development environment.

 What SAP Build Brings Together in summary -

Capability Description

App Development

Build responsive UI applications

Process Automation

Workflow, approvals, business rules

Workspaces

Business sites and launchpads

Pro-Code Extensions

CAP, APIs, advanced logic

AI Integration

Joule-powered assistance

SAP Build introduces a layered architecture, separating UI, process, and backend — enabling scalability and maintainability.

Use Case: Let us take one example and understand better.

Purchase Requisition is the most common Application

Let’s examine a simple yet powerful scenario:

Business Flow:

  1. Employee creates a purchase request
  2. Manager reviews the request
  3. Approval or rejection decision
  4. Status tracking

SAP Build Apps vs SAP Build: A Practical Comparison

 Architecture & Capability Comparison

Criteria SAP Build Apps SAP Build

Parameter

SAP Build Apps

SAP Build

Architecture

Flat (UI + logic combined)

Layered (UI + Workflow + Services)

Development Speed

Very fast

Moderate

Ease of Use

High (citizen developer friendly)

Medium

Workflow Capability

Manual logic

Built-in workflow engine

Process Handling

Simulated

Real orchestration

Integration

Limited

Strong SAP + API integration

Scalability

Low

Enterprise-grade

Maintainability

Complex for large apps

Modular and structured

Extensibility

Limited

Pro-code ready

AI Capabilities

Minimal

Integrated AI features

Future Readiness

Deprecated

Strategic platform

Deep Dive: Application Behavior

 SAP Build Apps Approach

  • Purchase Request is created via UI
  • Status of the purchase request is updated manually
  • Logic embedded within the app

Limitation:
No real workflow, no auditability, no scalability

SAP Build Approach

  • Request triggers workflow
  • Manager receives approval task
  • Decision updates system automatically

Advantage:
Process-driven, auditable, scalable, enterprise-ready

Conclusion

SAP Build Apps was instrumental in enabling rapid innovation and empowering citizen developers.

However, as enterprise requirements evolved, SAP Build emerged as the strategic platform, offering:

  • End-to-end process orchestration
  • Seamless integration
  • Scalable architecture
  • AI-powered development

The future is not just about building apps —
It is about building intelligent, integrated business solutions.

entry.title: Re: Extraction results via Import Export Data changed
entry.content:
Please confirm if you are referring to the job response file generated after the import.

Kindly review the results in that file for validation purposes. However, please note that this file should not be used for re-import. Any required corrections should be made in the original/main import file instead.

Best Practice:

  • Always use the exported file as a template
  • Validate and update the data carefully
  • Proceed with the import using the corrected main file

entry.title: Re: Addition of Column in Standard Report Display Purchas...
entry.content: You cannot add a new column directly to ME2M in S/4HANA Public Cloud. The supported approach is to use Custom Analytical Queries or Custom Fields and Logic to build a variant report with the required field.

entry.title: How to use BAPI to create ODATA service
entry.content:
Introduction:
BAPIs are standardized SAP methods used to access business data, and they can be exposed as OData services via SAP Gateway to enable integration with web and mobile applications. BAPIs are commonly used for performing operations such as creating, updating, or retrieving business data.

Note: I'm assuming to have BAPI and ODATA config along with well-known with BAPI and ODATA T-codes.

Step 1: Use RFC to import in data model for creating ODATA service.

Provide RFC and Entity name.

Step 2: Provide the BAPI name and make sure default entity set check box is marked.

Field selection

Step 3: Select all the possible fields you wanted to use from RFC BAPI structure.

Mark Key field

Step 4: provide the key field from the selected fields.
Step 5: To automatically generate code to get entity set data. we have to map to data source.

Step 6: Again, provide the BAPI RFC name for map to data source.

Propose Mapping

Step 7: To create mapping between BAPI and source structure we have to do propose mapping. Which will automatically handle this mapping.
Step 8: Once all these steps are created at last you have to generate runtime objects and this will create the highlighted object from above screen shot.

Step 9: Finally, you have to run the object in T-code “/IWFND/GW_CLIENT” for created entity set. You will receive the data.

Conclusion:
Using BAPIs in OData services ensures reuse of reliable SAP logic while providing a scalable and efficient way to connect SAP systems with external applications.

entry.title: Real Estate Cost Settlement
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

We would like to use the Real Estate Cost Settlement to settle costs to our tenants. In a starter public cloud system we activated the scope item 6N8. When trying to create service groups, we noticed that it is a must to have a building / land object to assign to the service group. We cannot create these objects in our system, but we are able to create usable- and rentable objects.

Does this mean that we can only use this Real Estate Cost Settlement if we have integration with a master data system like Planon? We are not planning to use such a master data system, but we would like to create a cost settlement.

Thanks in advance


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Real Estate Cost Settlement
entry.content:
Thank you for your question!

To process Cost Settlements for real estate objects, there are certain prerequisites that must be met. These prerequisites are outlined in the test script for scope item 6N8 - available on the SAP Process Navigator. I highly recommend reviewing them before starting any form of testing, especially as these test scripts outline a best practice approach for the solution which can be of help during initial testing phase.

Indeed, the setup of Architecture Objects is a prerequisite for cost settlement service groups. In addition, Master Data Integration setup to a third party application (i.e. Planon) is also a prerequisite which is highlighted in the test script as well. The relevant "scope item" for that is 77V which must be set up before proceeding with the testing of 6N8.

Real Estate Cost Settlement process is only usable when these prerequisites are also considered - I can also recommend the SAP Help Portal article which explains the overall end-to-end process for cost settlements. An FAQ is also available on this page which can provide even more context to help in your testing efforts.

entry.title: Establishing Corporate Carbon Footprint Emissions with Advanced Configurations
entry.content:
Why Advanced Carbon Management Matters

As sustainability requirements grow, basic carbon calculations are no longer enough. Organizations need deeper insights into their emissions across operations, supply chains, and product lifecycles.

SAP Sustainability Footprint Management enables advanced carbon footprint calculations by integrating business data, applying intelligent mapping, and analysing emissions across all relevant scopes.

With advanced capabilities, businesses can:

  • Calculate emissions across complex Scope 1, 2, and 3 scenarios
  • Integrate data from ERP and external systems
  • Leverage AI for emission factor mapping
  • Identify emission hotspots and reduction opportunities
  • Drive targeted decarbonization strategies

What You’ll Learn in This Learning Journey

This in-depth, 3-hour course builds on foundational knowledge and introduces advanced carbon management capabilities:

  1. System Integration
    Learn how to integrate SAP Sustainability Footprint Management with ERP systems to access relevant business data.
  2. Energy Flow Modelling
    Model energy consumption and calculate emissions for Scope 1, Scope 2, and selected Scope 3 categories using advanced energy flow logic.
  3. Supplier and Purchased Goods Emissions (Scope 3.1)
    Use AI-based mapping and integrate supplier data, including via SAP Sustainability Data Exchange, to calculate emissions from purchased goods and services.
  4. Transport Emissions (Scope 3.4)
    Configure and calculate emissions related to logistics and transportation activities.
  5. Waste Emissions (Scope 3.5)
    Track and calculate emissions resulting from waste management processes.
  6. Product Use Emissions (Scope 3.11)
    Assess emissions generated during the use phase of sold products and allocate them correctly.
  7. Financial Integration
    Publish carbon data to SAP Green Ledger for financial and reporting alignment.
  8. Identifying Hotspots
    Analyse emissions data to identify key drivers and uncover actionable decarbonization levers.

Who Should Take the Course?

This learning journey is ideal for:

  • Data Analysts working with sustainability data
  • Consultants implementing advanced carbon management solutions
  • Business Users analyzing emissions and performance
  • Administrators managing configuration and integration

Prerequisite: Knowledge from the Learning Journey Establishing Corporate Carbon Footprint Emissions with Simple Configuration.

Take your carbon management to the next level and explore the Learning Journey “Establishing Corporate Carbon Footprint Emissions with Advanced Configuration” here.

entry.title: Best Practice für Automatic Implementation of SAP Notes (Transportreigabe)
entry.content:
Beim Update (SUM) wird die Option "Shadow System Development User" im Step "Required Shadow Parameters (PREP_INSTALL/SUBMOD_PREP_INSTALL/INITSHD) per Haken "Yes, SUM shall create a shadow system development user" verwendet.

Bei dem Vorgang wird auch die Funktion "Automated Reset of Obsolete SAP Notes" durchgeführt und auf einen Transport geschrieben. -> https://help.sap.com/docs/SLTOOLSET/b0d775c8a9fd47b985a5e66ddba31d33/46ebfbd34e3c46918ab73cc6919971f2.html#automated-reset-of-obsolete-sap-notes

Laut https://me.sap.com/notes/0003727019 schlägt die SAP vor, den TR nach dem Update freizugeben. Weiterhin wird von einem Transport ins Folgesystem abgeraten, sondern man solle dort erneut die gleiche Option (Yes, SUM shall create a shadow system development user) wie im Vorsystem ausführen .

Beim aktuellen Updateprozess lief der SPAU in eine Sperrsituation, weil Objekte auf dem besagten Transport standen. Da dies nur obsolete Hinweise betrifft, ist dies nicht kritisch.

Aber so stellt sich nun die Frage, wann genau der richtige Zeitpunkt für die Freigabe des Transports ist. Wie oben beschrieben, sollte das nach dem Update passieren. Unserer Meinung nach, wäre der richtige Zeitpunkt, vor SPAU.

Wann ist der tatsächlich richtige Zeitpunkt für die Freigabe des Transports?
